elements.sent-2411-001: By applying some techniques of set-valued and variational analysis, we study solution stability of nonhomogeneous split equality problems and nonhomogeneous split feasibility problems, where the constraint sets need not be convex.
elements.sent-2411-002: Necessary and sufficient conditions for the Lipschitz-likeness of the solution maps of the problems are given and illustrated by concrete examples.
elements.sent-2411-003: The obtained results complement those given in~[Huong VT, Xu HK, Yen ND.
elements.sent-2411-004: Since the problem is also useful in signal processing/imaging reconstruction, medical treatment of intensity-modulated radiation therapy, gene regulatory network inference, etc., it has been studied by many authors; see, e.g.,~\cite{HuongXuYen22 for some related references.}
elements.sent-2411-005: Apart from (SFP), another feasibility problem called the split equality problem (SEP), which was proposed by Moudafi and his coauthors~\cite{Moudafi13,Moudafi14,Moudafi_Shemas13,Byrne_Moudafi_17 , can be used in decomposition methods for PDEs, game theory, and intensity-modulated radiation therapy.}
elements.sent-2411-006: Relationships between (SEP) and (SFP) can be seen in~\cite[Section~3]{Xu_Celgieski21 .}
elements.sent-2411-007: The study of (SEP) and (SFP) is mainly focused on solution algorithms for (SFP).
elements.sent-2411-008: The interested reader is referred to~\cite{HuongXuYen22 for several references and comments.}
elements.sent-2411-009: In~\cite{HuongXuYen22 , we have shown that solution stability of the above feasibility problems can be successfully investigated by set-valued and variational analysis techniques.}
elements.sent-2411-010: The main idea is to transform the problems into suitable parametric generalized equations and use the machinery of generalized differentiation from~\cite{B-M06 , as well as a fundamental result of Mordukhovich~\cite{Mor04 .}}
elements.sent-2411-011: The results obtained in~\cite{HuongXuYen22 include necessary and sufficient conditions for the Lipschitz-like property of the solution map in question.}
elements.sent-2411-012: A discussion on the importance of the latter property in and its relations to other stability property of set-valued maps can be found in~\cite{HuongXuYen22 .}
elements.sent-2411-013: Introducing a canonical perturbation, usually termed the right-hand-side perturbation, to the constraint system of (SEP) (resp., of (SFP)), we get a nonhomogeneous split equality problem (resp.,~nonhomogeneous split feasibility problem).
elements.sent-2411-014: The first model has been considered by Reich and Tuyen~\cite{Reich_Tuyen_21 .}
elements.sent-2411-015: It is worth emphasizing that the consideration of nonhomogeneous systems is a traditional issue in mathematics.
elements.sent-2411-016: For instance, in parallel to homogeneous systems of linear equations (resp., homogeneous systems of linear differential equations), one also considers nonhomogeneous systems of linear equations (resp., nonhomogeneous systems of linear differential equations).
elements.sent-2411-017: Our aim in this paper is to characterize the Lipschitz-like property of the solution map of the nonhomogeneous split equality problem and of the nonhomogeneous split feasibility problem where the constraint sets are not required to be convex.
elements.sent-2411-018: The aim will be achieved by developing some proof schemes used in~\cite{HuongXuYen22 .}
elements.sent-2411-019: Due to the appearance of the canonical perturbation (the right-hand-side perturbation), the obtained results cannot be derived from those given in~\cite{HuongXuYen22 .}
elements.sent-2411-020: Conversely, the characterizations of the Lipschitz-like property of the solution maps in the paper do not imply the ones of~\cite{HuongXuYen22 .}
elements.sent-2411-021: The paper organization is as follows.
